title: ceropteris sulphurea
intro: Ceropteris sulphurea, also known as the Sulphur Lady Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae and is native to parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Ceropteris sulphurea has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional.
